items.title: One Nation candidate published antisemitic, racist and homophobic posts

children.content: A One Nation candidate in the South Australian election was accused of domestic abuse, bullying and harassment, while another made racist, antisemitic and homophobic social media posts, raising fresh questions about the party's vetting processes as it surges in popularity.

gallery.altText: A combination of two images, each showing a man in a suit smiling in front of a soft green backdrop.

thumbnail.altText: A combination of two images, each showing a man in a suit smiling in front of a soft green backdrop.

items.title: Woman killed by bus in Adelaide CBD in third pedestrian crash in three days

children.content: Witnesses are being encouraged to seek counselling after a 32-year-old died when she was hit by an Adelaide Metro bus on King William Road in the Adelaide CBD. It comes after two other pedestrians were hit and injured in similar incidents earlier in the week.

image.altText: A red Adelaide Metro bus on a road in Adelaide's CBD.

items.title: Adelaide marks one year of no cars crashing on O-Bahn, in possible record

children.content: For possibly the first time since it opened 40 years ago, the Adelaide busway has remained clear of crashed cars, in a record to be celebrated live on air.

image.altText: A hatchback crashed on a concrete bus while a bus goes in the other direction

items.title: More GPs charging administration fees to cover payroll tax changes in SA

children.content: Patients are being asked to make an additional, separate transaction at general practices across Adelaide of between $5 and $10 to cover increased operating costs following the implementation of payroll tax for contracted doctors in 2024.

image.altText: A doctor wearing a white coat and a stethoscope writes on a clipboard.

items.title: Trial pours cold water on current EV truck viability despite diesel price hike

children.content: A 2.5-year trial of an electric truck in SA's forestry industry has concluded with the truck now parked, finding it was able to do the job but wasn't cost effective, even with high fuel prices.

image.altText: A woman wearing a white shirt and glasses looks as the camera, with a lage blue truck in the background.

items.title: Outback SA operator says Easter tourism 'worse than COVID', while others see spike

children.content: An Ikara-Flinders Ranges tourism operator says he is experiencing more cancellations than bookings this Easter as fuel shortage anxiety keep travellers away, while other regional towns expect to be booked out.

gallery.altText: A car drives into the sunset near the Flinders Ranges.

thumbnail.altText: A car drives into the sunset near the Flinders Ranges.

items.title: Sixers force NBL title decider after beating Kings in cliffhanger

children.content: Torrey Craig's missed free throw for the Sydney Kings in the dying seconds allows the Adelaide 36ers to level the NBL championship series at 2-2 with a gripping 92-91 win.

image.altText: Two basketball players in action, one in a white uniform and one in a navy uniform

items.title: Duo charged after police find $320,000 allegedly stolen Lego haul

children.content: A man and woman will face court after allegedly stealing hundreds of Lego sets from retail outlets across metropolitan Adelaide.

image.altText: Several boxes of Lego on pallets in a large shed

items.title: Fears for stingray populations as algal bloom moves north

children.content: Evidence of South Australia's harmful algal bloom has been detected in previously untouched parts of the Spencer Gulf days after rays were seen gathering in shallow waters.

gallery.altText: A dead stingray lying in a small puddle among rocks, with its white belly facing up

thumbnail.altText: A dead stingray lying in a small puddle among rocks, with its white belly facing up

items.title: SA primary school principals' association query free public school policy

children.content: A peak body for primary school principals says it is worried abolishing public school fees will see more money flow to wealthier schools, but the SA education minister says all parents were "doing it tough" and federal funding would go to addressing inequality.

image.altText: A composite image of a man and a woman looking at each other

items.title: 'It's tragic': Hundreds to be impacted after seafood giant folds

children.content: Australia's largest wild-caught prawn operation is to close its doors after 60 years in business, impacting hundreds of workers and devastating a Queensland community.

image.altText: raptis fleets docked at the karumba wharf

items.title: Free dental clinic changes lives in cost-of-living crisis

children.content: Demand is high for an Adelaide dental program that offers free services to patients who have experienced homelessness or who have trouble accessing conventional care.

image.altText: A woman smiles as she holds a guitar across her lap, sitting in a park

items.title: Shooters deployed to curb Kangaroo Island's big little corella problem

children.content: Thermal imaging, infrared technology and crack marksmanship are deployed to battle the island's growing little corella population.

image.altText: A little corella in a tree with its wings slightly open

items.title: Sit back and let us take you on a journey to the moon with Artemis II

children.content: When a mission like Artemis II launches, most of the excitement happens in the first few minutes. What should we be looking out for?

gallery.altText: A 3D wireframe rocket with 3 male and 1 female astronauts pose with looming large moon behind

thumbnail.altText: A 3D wireframe rocket with 3 male and 1 female astronauts pose with looming large moon behind

items.title: Albanese will today try to defend his prime-time message

children.content: With no realistic end in sight and a whole lot of economic pain to come, the PM's decision to make a direct plea to the nation was not one taken lightly.

image.altText: A man in a suit looks across the camera with an australian flag in the background.

items.title: PM unveils gambling ad reforms, three years after landmark report

children.content: Speaking at the National Press Club, Anthony Albanese announces new reforms more than 1,000 days after the government received a report from an inquiry into the harm caused by online gambling and gambling advertising.

image.altText: Albanese speaks at National Press Club

items.title: Thousands lost to cancellations as fuel crisis bites southern Qld tourism sector

children.content: Tourism businesses say they’re bearing the brunt of surging fuel prices, with some losing thousands to booking cancellations and extra operational costs, while others field back-to-back calls from anxious tourists about fuel availability.

articles.title: 'Domino effect': Why Singapore's oil shock will play out in Australia

children.content: Singapore is exposed to the oil shock from Iran's effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, and its response will shape the fuel crisis in Australia.

image.altText: Chimneys and white storage tankers next to a bay of grey water.

articles.title: What Australia can learn from French cities that made public transport free

children.content: There are growing calls in Australia for free public transport amid the Iran war with two states putting in place free services on a temporary basis. But in France, almost 50 cities have free public transport.

image.altText: Montpellier tramway with a message promoting free public transport.

gallery.altText: Three tourists looking out at the ocean while on a ferry.

thumbnail.altText: Three tourists looking out at the ocean while on a ferry.

items.title: Track the latest petrol and diesel prices around Australia

children.content: See what's happening with petrol and diesel prices near you, updated constantly.

items.title: Powerful magnitude-7.4 earthquake strikes Indonesia, tsunami warning issued

children.content: The quake, which hit early today, had a depth of 35 kilometres and its epicentre was 127km west-north-west of Ternate, Indonesia, the US Geological Survey said.

image.altText: An aerial view of islands around Raja Ampat in east Indonesia's West Papua region

items.title: International crypto firm targets uni students despite ASIC ban

children.content: A major cryptocurrency platform is operating like "gambling" in Australia, according to experts, despite new regulatory laws.

image.altText: Two large gold coins bearing engraved "b"s lie on top of a complex financial line-and-bar graph

items.title: ASX unimpressed by Trump speech

children.content: ASX drops back following President Donald Trump's speech on Iran. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: A woman walks near an ASX stock exchange board.

items.title: Bondi gunman fails to have family's identities suppressed for 40 years

children.content: Lawyers for Bondi gunman Naveed Akram fail in a bid to have the identities of his mother, brother and sister protected by a decades-long court suppression order on safety grounds.

image.altText: Naveed Akram

items.title: How to nurture your non-work identity

children.content: The centrality of work in our lives can't be denied, especially when Australians are working longer hours than ever before. But short of quitting our jobs, how do we shift the balance?

gallery.altText: An illustration of a woman working at a check-out, but looking into a mirror and playing the guitar.

thumbnail.altText: An illustration of a woman working at a check-out, but looking into a mirror and playing the guitar.

items.title: Here is the key to getting through The Super Mario Galaxy Movie

children.content: Even with downgraded expectations, the Nintendo sequel is little more than Easter eggs and bright colours. But that won't stop it from filling cinemas.

image.altText: A shot from Super Mario Galaxy Movie featuring Mario, Luigi, Peach and Yoshi

items.title: Museum fans: Test yourself with our curious collection of trivia

children.content: Australia’s museums hold a treasure trove of odd facts, native animals and even burlesque history. So how much do you know about the things hidden in our nation’s vaults?

image.altText: A close up of a plastic dinosaur.

items.title: Australian-based Harrods survivor was 'fresh game' for Mohamed Al Fayed

children.content: Jessica tried to keep her distance from Harrods billionaire owner Mohamed Al Fayed, who was known to abuse his female staff, until he spotted her going down an escalator.

gallery.altText: A woman looks directly at the camera.
A close up image of the face of Mohamed Al Fayed wearing a grey blazer jacket
A photograph of a young woman looking at the camera with her hand on her head.

thumbnail.altText: A woman looks directly at the camera.
A close up image of the face of Mohamed Al Fayed wearing a grey blazer jacket
A photograph of a young woman looking at the camera with her hand on her head.

items.title: Gas exports face halt as winter shortages loom

children.content: It is the first time the government has taken a step towards using the powers it has to limit exports, and it comes at a time when the profits from those exports appear set to surge.

image.altText: The PAN Europe in the ocean with mountains in the background.

items.title: Weather to deliver Easter treat, but another cyclone looms next week

children.content: Australia has just been soaked by its third wettest February-March on record, but for most of the country, skies have cleared up just in time for Easter.

image.altText: A map of Australia with expected rain totals for the Easter long weekend, showing orange along the southern and eastern coasts.

items.title: 'Rigged game of chess': Universities refuse to reveal executive salary information

children.content: Victorian universities are abusing Freedom Of Information laws, according to one union, as transparency concerns grow. The claim follows a Four Corners investigation that revealed universities spent $1.8 billion on consultants in one year.

image.altText: The exterior of a university building with silhoutted figures walking nearby.

items.title: Storm forward leaves hospital after suffering suspected stroke

children.content: The club says Tui Kamikamica will now "commence a period of inpatient rehabilitation" after the "medical episode" on Monday.

image.altText: Tui Kamikamica playing for the Melbourne Storm during a 2025 NRL match.

items.title: DNA links US serial killer Ted Bundy to unsolved 1974 murder

children.content: New DNA testing definitively links infamous serial killer Ted Bundy to the unsolved death of a US teenager in 1974.

gallery.altText: A black and white photo of a man in a suit with a white shirt and black tie, looking directly into the camera.

thumbnail.altText: A black and white photo of a man in a suit with a white shirt and black tie, looking directly into the camera.

items.title: Australia wasting migrant talent on an 'industrial scale', experts say

children.content: Australia is failing to recognise the qualifications of highly trained permanent migrants, former Treasury secretary Martin Parkinson says.

image.altText: A worker wearing a protective helmet welds a girder.

items.title: 'Cameras watching': Horrifying trend of technology-based abuse

children.content: One in three women on temporary visas are experiencing domestic and family violence, safety advocates say.

image.altText: A professional with glasses and a sombre expression.

items.title: Why domestic violence social workers are leaving the field in droves

children.content: In the past year alone, NSW-based domestic and family violence services have seen a 22 per cent increase in new referrals — but in the meantime the workforce has shrunk by 12 per cent.

image.altText: Michelle Lin Sydney social worker

items.title: Woods turns down Ryder Cup captaincy as he seeks treatment outside of US

children.content: Tiger Woods declines the chance to captain the United States in next year's Ryder Cup after a Florida judge approved his motion to leave the country to seek "comprehensive inpatient treatment".

image.altText: Tiger Woods of the Jupiter Links Golf Club plays a shot from a bunker
